summary: Drew DeVault provides a progress update on Sway, the i3-compatible Wayland compositor, as of April 2017. The project has grown to over 21,000 lines of C from 81 contributors, with recent releases adding redshift support, binary space partitioning layouts, and nvidia driver support. A bounty program has raised $1,200, with awardees notably redonating their rewards back to the pool. The most significant long-term development is wlroots, a new compositor plumbing library to replace wlc, which promises to fix deep bugs and enable new features across the Wayland ecosystem.
key_points: Sway 0.12 added redshift support and BSP layouts; 0.13 brings nvidia proprietary driver support
The project has 81 contributors, 2,263 commits, and is packaged in most major Linux distributions
A bounty program raised $1,200, with every awardee choosing to redonate their bounty back to the pool
Work has begun on wlroots, a replacement for wlc that will enable standard protocols other Wayland compositors can implement
wlroots aims to unify the Wayland platform by providing shared compositor infrastructure
money_quotes: I thought we would have slowed down a lot more by now, but every release still comes with new features.
It's i3, but for Wayland, plus it's got some bonus features.
Every awardee chose to redonate their reward to the bounty pool.
Bounties have also been awarded for features in the Wayland ecosystem beyond Sway - a fact I'm especially proud of.
We've also started work on a long term project to replace our compositor plumbing library, wlc, with a new one: wlroots.
This should allow us to fix many of the more difficult bugs in Sway, and opens the doors for many features that weren't previously possible.
It should also give us a platform on which we can build standard protocols that other compositors can implement, unifying the Wayland platform a bit more.
key_insight: Sway's development is accelerating rather than slowing, with the ambitious wlroots project poised to benefit not just Sway but the entire Wayland ecosystem through shared compositor infrastructure.
